verdict.lens: Pipeline-value skeptic: does the 20-step pipeline add real value over "Claude + WebSearch + one good prompt"?

strengths.claim: The adversarial critics do real, non-faked work that a single-prompt baseline structurally cannot: they search the on-disk vault for counter-evidence that exists but is missing from the draft.
strengths.evidence: hooks.py:590-595 (dialectic-critic) instructs `search "<keyword>" --tag <vault_tag>` to find interim/width notes that 'disagree with or complicate the draft's claims', then read suspect notes in full. depth-critic (hooks.py:701-706) lists `--type interim` notes and checks the draft actually USES their quotes/numbers. This is grounded in gathered evidence, not the model re-reading its own output. A naive Claude+WebSearch run has no separate corpus to audit the draft against.

strengths.claim: The patch-never-regenerate architecture is genuinely enforced at the tool level, not just by prose, which is the real defense against an LLM laundering hallucinations through a 'fix'.
strengths.evidence: hooks.py:1396-1404 patcher agent is `tools: Read, Edit` (no Write, no Bash); patcher.md:50 has the orchestrator pre-stub patch-log.json precisely because the patcher 'cannot Write'. The invariant (bad-research.md:354) makes critics emit findings and only the Read+Edit patcher touch substance. This is a real structural guarantee, not ceremony.

strengths.claim: The decompose step's coverage-matrix self-audit and required_section_headings are real instruction-following machinery that demonstrably maps to the widest-variance failure mode (structural mirror), and is regression-tested.
strengths.evidence: decompose.md:182-199 forces a phrase-by-phrase query->atomic-item matrix with a 'zero Gap?=YES rows' exit criterion; decompose.md:68 mandates a non-empty heading contract. classify_route is unit-tested via golden _check_decompose (golden.py:198-205). This catches the 'right topic, wrong structure / dropped sub-question' failure that one-shot prompting routinely commits.

strengths.claim: The Tier-A byte-identity citation check is a real, $0, keyless guarantee that catches fabricated quotes — the single most valuable grounding primitive and it is genuinely tested.
strengths.evidence: verifier.py:144-151 tier_a_byte_identity re-finds quoted_support at [char_start:char_end] AND SHA-matches anchor_id; the deterministic no-uncited-claim gate (gate.py:145-180) is a hard ship-block. 112 grounding tests pass (tests/test_grounding/ runs green). This is a concrete capability a naive baseline lacks entirely.

strengths.claim: The contradiction-graph -> loci -> depth -> tensions chain forces explicit dialectical engagement that one-shot prompting skips, and the dynamic source-budget scoring is real (not a flat fan-out).
strengths.evidence: loci-analysis.md:109-122 scores each locus on importance/uncertainty/disagreement/decision_impact (max 40) and allocates 0-15 sources proportionally; an INVARIANT (line 154) requires >=1 dialectical locus or a justified skip. cross-locus-reconcile.md:121-125 forces a pre-committed resolution per tension. This is structured argument-construction, not reformatting.

weaknesses.claim: The advertised 'backward grounding / NLI entailment' citation verifier is a NO-OP on the default keyless path — the exact configuration the whole product is built around. It cannot catch a paraphrase that flips a number.
weaknesses.evidence: verifier.py CitationPresentNLI.predict() (lines 38-41) returns {entailment:1.0} for ANY (premise,hypothesis); default_nli (lines 116-134) only returns the real CrossEncoderNLI when `[local]` (torch) is installed, otherwise LineSpanJudge/CitationPresentNLI. LineSpanJudge ACCEPTS any claim with >=0.8 lexical overlap to its span WITHOUT a check (verifier.py:75-79), and gate.py:198-205 admits in-comment the residual 'grew 12% vs span saying grew 21%' number-flip 'is a known keyless gap'. test_verifier.py:200-213 confirms: 'the no-op marks any premise/hypothesis as entailment'. The drift-CONTRADICTED test (line 385) only passes because it injects a test-double HypothesisAwareNLI, NOT the keyless default. So 'every factual claim bound to a source' (skill description) means 'a quote string exists and byte-matches' — not 'the source actually supports the claim' — unless the user installs torch or wires a host judge for the paraphrase band.

weaknesses.claim: The pass_rate 1.0 golden eval is self-graded by a trivial deterministic rubric and is worthless as evidence the pipeline is good. It is circular: the system grades itself with checks any competent report passes.
weaknesses.evidence: golden-eval-report.json: all 8 cases, all 5 rails 'pass', every rationale 'deterministic offline rubric'. golden.py:150-194 shows the rubric is: citation=does each line contain a [cite] regex; factual=>=20% word-overlap with corpus AND no banned overclaim word; completeness=>=1 body line; source_quality=URLs contain '//'. These are presence checks, not quality checks — a mediocre report passes all of them. No external benchmark, no human grade, no held-out judge.

weaknesses.claim: The headline quantitative justification for the entire 20-step architecture — 'the difference between the full pipeline (55.9) and the single-draft fallback (52.6)' — has zero provenance in code, tests, or eval and traces only to internal narrative references ('Q57'/'Q9').
weaknesses.evidence: grep for 55.9/52.6 across the repo hits exactly two places: the marketing line in bad-research.md:373 and docs/investigation/01_FOUNDATION.md:60 which reveals the numbers are 'Q57 (55.9, full pipeline) and Q9 (52.6, single-draft fallback)' — internal question IDs, not a reproducible benchmark. docs/enhancements/COMPETITIVE_DR_SYSTEMS.md:465 itself flags 'Benchmark claim self-reported/unverified'. The 2.5x time cost of 'full' (1.5-2.5h, README:56) rests on a 3.3-point delta nobody can reproduce.

weaknesses.claim: The shipped agent definitions are stale relative to the current skill architecture — they still describe a '7-phase / Layer' pipeline and read research/comparisons.md, an artifact the skills renamed to research/temp/tensions.md. The critics may look for inputs that no longer exist.
weaknesses.evidence: hooks.py:549,561 (dialectic-critic) and 672,1408 (depth-critic, patcher) all say 'Layer 5/6 of the 7-phase pipeline' and cite 'cross-locus reconciliation (Layer 3.5 — research/comparisons.md)'. But step 6 (cross-locus-reconcile.md:16) explicitly states comparisons.md was MERGED into research/temp/tensions.md. The orchestrator-facing skills say 20 steps; the worker agents say 7 layers. This drift means a critic told to read comparisons.md finds nothing.

weaknesses.claim: Several 'rigor' sub-steps are token-burning ceremony whose output the downstream steps cannot meaningfully consume — notably the 6-dimension 0-3 utility scoring and the evidence-redundancy audit, which produce precise-looking numbers from pure model intuition with no verification.
weaknesses.evidence: width-sweep.md:144-159 has the model assign Authority/Novelty/Stance/Coverage/Redundancy/Freshness scores (0-3 each, 'max composite 18') to every URL by eyeball, then 'rank by composite' — but step 2.2 PREFERRED path hands everything to `bad funnel-gather` which does its own deterministic rank/rerank (width-sweep.md:93-121), making the hand-scored table dead scaffolding on the funnel path. The redundancy audit (2.6) and confidence-band hedging (patcher 14.2b) similarly manufacture quantitative-looking artifacts (verify_score x fetcher-confidence x n_sources) on top of the verify_score that, per the weakness above, is itself a near-no-op on the keyless default.

top_improvements.title: Make the keyless faithfulness check actually check faithfulness for the high-stakes band (numbers/dates), or stop advertising 'backward grounding'.
top_improvements.rationale: The product's core promise is 'every factual claim bound to a source,' but on the default keyless path the entailment lane is a no-op and the >=0.8-overlap band (where number-flips live) is auto-accepted. Route every MUST-verify sentence (numbers, dates, prices, 'current/latest') through the batched host judge unconditionally — even at >=0.8 overlap — since the host model IS already wired and that band is exactly where a flipped digit hides. This is the difference between real grounding and citation-theater.

top_improvements.title: Replace the self-graded deterministic golden eval with a held-out LLM-judge (or human) score, and stop citing the unsourced 55.9/52.6 delta.
top_improvements.rationale: pass_rate 1.0 from a presence-checking rubric is not evidence; it is a number that will always be 1.0. Either run the real RACE-style judge the docs reference against a frozen set with the single-prompt baseline as the control, or remove the '55.9 vs 52.6' justification from the user-facing skill. Right now the entire 2.5-hour architecture is justified by an unreproducible 3.3-point internal number.

top_improvements.title: Regenerate the worker-agent definitions in hooks.py from the current skill architecture (kill the '7-phase/Layer' and comparisons.md references).
top_improvements.rationale: Critics and the patcher ship with stale pipeline-position prose and point at a renamed artifact (comparisons.md -> tensions.md). A critic that searches for an input that no longer exists silently does less work. Single source of truth: derive agent prompts from the same step taxonomy the orchestrator uses.

top_improvements.title: Cut or gate the eyeball-scored utility table and redundancy audit on the funnel path.
top_improvements.rationale: When `bad funnel-gather` does deterministic rank/rerank, the hand-assigned 0-3 six-dimension scores are dead ceremony that burns Opus tokens to produce numbers nothing consumes. Either feed the scores INTO the funnel ranking or skip them when the funnel runs. Honesty about which steps are load-bearing vs decorative would shrink the run and the token bill.

verdict.bottom_line: The pipeline is roughly two-thirds real and one-third theater, and the theater is concentrated exactly where the marketing is loudest. The genuinely valuable parts are structural and impossible to fake in a one-shot prompt: tool-locked Read+Edit critics that search an on-disk corpus for counter-evidence the draft omitted, a patch-never-regenerate guarantee enforced at the tool level, a coverage-matrix decomposition that targets the real instruction-following failure mode, and a $0 byte-identity quote check that does kill fabricated quotes — all backed by 112 passing grounding tests. That bundle plausibly beats naive Claude+WebSearch on contested, multi-source, structure-sensitive questions. But the flagship claim — 'backward grounding, every factual claim bound to a source' — is materially overstated on the default keyless path, where the NLI entailment lane is an admitted no-op and near-verbatim number-flips are auto-accepted; real semantic faithfulness only exists if you install torch or wire the host judge into the high-overlap band, which the skill doesn't make you do. The pass_rate 1.0 eval is self-graded by a presence-checking rubric and proves nothing, and the '55.9 vs 52.6' figure that justifies the whole 2.5-hour edifice has no reproducible source. So: a strong, defensible research scaffold wrapped in an evidence layer that is partly aspirational and an eval layer that is circular. Fix the keyless faithfulness check and replace the self-grade with a real judge and the value score jumps; until then, trust the critics and the decomposition, distrust the grounding badge and the benchmark numbers.
verdict.competitor_notes: Versus Gemini/OpenAI/Perplexity Deep Research and Claude's own research mode: bad-research's distinctive WIN is the explicit adversarial layer (5 parallel critics that audit the draft against a separate evidence vault) plus the hard patch-never-regenerate discipline — commercial DR tools synthesize in one pass and rarely re-audit against retrieved corpus with this rigor; the docs correctly note Anthropic's CitationAgent has no faithfulness check and OpenAI's is RL-internal/un-portable (citation-verifier.md:88-89), so the byte-identity gate is a genuine differentiator IF the semantic lane were live. The clear LOSE is verifiability: Gemini/OpenAI/Perplexity report on public benchmarks (DeepResearch-Bench RACE, etc.) with third-party-checkable numbers, whereas bad-research's only quantitative self-assessment is a self-graded pass_rate 1.0 and an unsourced 55.9/52.6 — so on 'prove it's good' it loses badly. Mixed: latency — 1.5-2.5h full runs are far slower than commercial DR's 5-30 min, justified only by that unreproducible 3.3-point delta. Net: architecturally competitive on rigor and citation discipline, behind on proof and speed, and its headline grounding guarantee is weaker in the default config than a casual reader of the skill description would believe.

checks.claim: The advertised 'backward grounding / NLI entailment' citation verifier is a NO-OP on the default keyless path — the exact configuration the whole product is built around. It cannot catch a paraphrase that flips a number.
checks.evidence:
SUBSTANCE CONFIRMED, but the auditor's mechanism is imprecise in 3 ways; the real gap is actually worse than they say.

WHAT IS CONFIRMED:
1. CitationPresentNLI.predict() (verifier.py:38-41) returns {entailment:1.0, neutral:0.0, contradiction:0.0} for ANY (premise,hypothesis) — a true no-op. classify_nli (nli.py:28-32) then yields ENTAILMENT. Confirmed by test_default_nli_is_citation_present_stub_when_local_absent (test_grounding/test_verifier.py:200-213, comment line 210: "The no-op marks any premise/hypothesis as entailment"). All 24 verifier tests pass (ran: pytest -o addopts="" -> 24 passed).
2. default_nli (verifier.py:116-134) returns the real CrossEncoderNLI ONLY when nli_available() (i.e. [local]/torch installed); otherwise LineSpanJudge (if llm given) or CitationPresentNLI (if llm is None).
3. The near-verbatim number-flip gap is REAL and admitted in-code at gate.py:196-205: "a >=0.8-overlap report sentence that flipped a number ('grew 12%' vs a span saying 'grew 21%') ... is a known keyless gap that the [local]/keyed lane closes." I reproduced it: a long sentence flipping 12.4 ms -> 99.9 ms has content-overlap 0.882 >= CLAIM_QUOTE_OVERLAP_SKIP(0.8), so LineSpanJudge.predict (verifier.py:106-113) returns entailment=1.0 with NO judge call. So "every factual claim bound to a source" degrades to "a near-verbatim quote exists" in this band.
4. The drift-CONTRADICTED test (test_verify_checks_report_sentence_not_stored_claim, lines 385-409) only passes because it injects HypothesisAwareNLI() (test double, line 396), NOT the keyless default. Confirmed.

WHERE THE AUDITOR IS IMPRECISE (why partially-true not confirmed):
A. The actual CLI keyless default is LineSpanJudge, NOT the blanket CitationPresentNLI no-op. cli/research.py:391-392 wires default_nli(llm=llm) with a non-None llm, so per verifier.py:132-133 the keyless path resolves to LineSpanJudge. CitationPresentNLI only fires when llm is None (verifier.py:134) — never in the CLI.
B. LineSpanJudge is NOT a blanket no-op: genuine paraphrases (overlap < 0.8) return NEUTRAL and DO escalate to a real Tier-C host judge (verifier.py:390-393, 423-430) whose prompt says "Numbers must match exactly" (JUDGE_SYSTEM verifier.py:193). I verified the SHORT "12% vs 21%" example actually overlaps 0.75 < 0.8 -> NEUTRAL -> escalates (would be caught). The no-op is real only in the >=0.8 near-verbatim band, not for all paraphrases.
C. Auditor cited "verifier.py:75-79" for LineSpanJudge's accept-without-check; that range is actually HostJudgeNLI (lines 44-81). LineSpanJudge is lines 84-113 (accept at 111-112). Same behavior, wrong line cite.

A BIGGER GAP THE AUDITOR MISSED (makes the weakness worse): On a genuinely keyless run, `bad verify-citations` cannot run AT ALL. cli/research.py:391 calls get_llm_provider("anthropic", config=cfg); AnthropicProvider.__init__ (llm/anthropic.py:48-53) RAISES RuntimeError("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set") when no key. I reproduced it empirically (unset key -> RuntimeError). There is NO host-model LLMProvider implementation — AnthropicProvider is the only concrete LLMProvider (llm/base.py + llm/anthropic.py; grep found no ClaudeCodeProvider). Even the "keyless host-model" ClaudeCodeReranker lazily falls back to get_llm_provider("anthropic") (retrieval/rerank.py _provider()). So the Tier-C escalation that WOULD catch paraphrases requires a key, contradicting the keyless premise; without a key the whole verify step errors out rather than degrading to the no-op.
checks.note: Net: the auditor's headline (keyless verifier does not do real semantic entailment; a number-flip can pass) is correct and matches an in-code admission (gate.py:196-205). But "NO-OP for any paraphrase" overstates it — LineSpanJudge escalates sub-0.8-overlap paraphrases to a real judge; the no-op is confined to the >=0.8 near-verbatim band (where CitationPresentNLI, the llm=None fallback, is the only true blanket no-op). Separately, the deeper problem is that the Python verify CLI requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and cannot run on a truly keyless box at all — the host-model LLMProvider the docs (HOW_IT_WORKS.md:104 "a host-model gate") imply is never implemented as a provider. Skills 11.5 (full) and bad-research-fast.md:174 (fast) both delegate verification to this key-requiring CLI, so this affects every route, not just full.

checks.claim: The pass_rate 1.0 golden eval is self-graded by a trivial deterministic rubric and is worthless as evidence the pipeline is good. It is circular: the system grades itself with presence checks any competent (even mediocre) report passes. No external benchmark, no human grade, no held-out judge.
checks.evidence:
I reproduced the artifact: running `evaluate_corpus(load_golden_corpus())` on the default keyless judge regenerates golden-eval-report.json exactly — pass_rate 1.0, 8 scored cases, and every single case rationale is the literal string "deterministic offline rubric" (set() over all 8 == {'deterministic offline rubric'}). The report is the committed output of `bad gate` (src/bad_research/cli/calibrate.py:198-199 writes golden-eval-report.json from report.to_dict()), and git tracks it at repo root.

The rubric (src/bad_research/calibrate/golden.py, RubricJudge.judge, lines 139-194) is exactly the presence checks the auditor described — I verified each against source:
- citation (L151-157): every non-heading body line must match _CITE = re.compile(r"\[\[[^\]]+\]\]|\[\d+\]") (L97). Pure regex-presence-per-line.
- factual (L160-169): no banned word in _OVERCLAIM = ("definitively","cures all",...) AND content-word overlap with corpus >= 0.20 (L166). A grounding-vocabulary-overlap proxy, not entailment.
- completeness (L172): len(body) >= 1 — literally "has at least one body line."
- source_quality (L175-181): all corpus URLs contain "//" with a non-empty host.
- efficiency (L184-189): pass unless body empty.
The auditor's per-check description matches the code verbatim (the only nit: they cited "150-194"; the judge method spans 139-194, rails computed 150-189 — substance exact).

The circularity/self-grading is admitted IN THE CODE, which strengthens the claim. The two hardest fixtures (09_cited_contradiction, 10_over_hedged_completeness) carry requires_llm:true and are SKIPPED on the keyless path (golden.py:289; I confirmed `skipped requires_llm: ['09_cited_contradiction','10_over_hedged_completeness']`, 10 fixtures on disk but only 8 scored). Fixture 10 self-documents the gap in its own expected_behavior: "RubricJudge passes (content-word overlap is sufficient, hedging language not in _OVERCLAIM list)" — i.e. an evasive, content-free hedge report PASSES the keyless rubric by design. The RubricJudge docstring (golden.py:130-137) explicitly disclaims scope: "this keyless gate guards judge + router + retrieval determinism and citation/grounding presence — not synthesis-correctness drift ... CANNOT catch a well-cited claim that *contradicts* its source." judge.py/constants.py headers repeatedly label it "OFFLINE/reporting only ... never a per-run gate."

No external benchmark / human grade / held-out judge backs the 1.0: the only stronger path is the opt-in --llm LLMJudge (judge.py:139-168), which is the HOST model grading the host model's own output (still self-graded, just non-deterministic), and it is NOT what produced the committed report.
checks.note: Two caveats for fairness, both already accounted for in the verdict. (1) The rubric is NOT a no-op stub-that-always-passes — it does discriminate (overclaim words, uncited lines, empty body would FAIL; this is real, see the contrasting 09/10 fixtures), so "trivial" is right but "does literally nothing" would be an overstatement the auditor did not make. (2) The system is honest about the limitation internally (docstrings + SPEC "reporting only, never a gate"). The remaining real problem the auditor correctly targets: the repo-root golden-eval-report.json presents "pass_rate": 1.0 and "synthesis": 1.0 (golden.py:300, derived from the SAME rubric rails, not any independent synthesis-quality signal) with NO caveat attached — so as a standalone marketing artifact it is circular and worthless as evidence the pipeline produces good reports, exactly as claimed.

checks.claim: The adversarial critics do real, non-faked work that a single-prompt baseline structurally cannot: they search the on-disk vault for counter-evidence that exists but is missing from the draft.
checks.evidence:
The auditor's two cited line ranges match verbatim. hooks.py:590-595 (dialectic-critic Procedure step 2) instructs `{hpr_path} search "<keyword>" --tag <vault_tag> -j` to "find interim notes, width-corpus notes, and source extracts that disagree with or complicate the draft's claims" and then read suspect notes in full via `note show <id> -j`. hooks.py:701-706 (depth-critic Procedure steps 1-2) instructs `{hpr_path} search "" --tag <vault_tag> --type interim -j` to list every depth-investigator interim note and then asks "is the Synthesis section of this note adequately reflected in the draft?" — i.e. auditing the draft against a separate corpus, not re-reading model output.

The underlying mechanism is REAL, not theater, and I verified it down to the implementation and tests:
1. The corpus the critics audit against is written to disk by a DIFFERENT earlier step. hooks.py:390-394 shows the depth-investigator (Layer 3) writes interim notes via `note new ... --type interim --body-file ...`. note.py:104-112 confirms `note new` does a genuine `file_path.write_text(content)` into notes_dir, so the corpus is on disk, independent of the critic's prompt context.
2. The `search` command is a genuine on-disk store query, not a stub. vault_cmds.py:241-318 (`search_cmd`) globs `notes_dir/*.md`, parses frontmatter, filters by `--tag` (line 282) and `--type` (line 284), and term-frequency-scores bodies for non-empty queries (lines 300-316). `--include-body` returns the frontmatter-stripped body (lines 327-333) — exactly the "read suspect notes in full" step.
3. Passing tests exercise every load-bearing path: tests/test_cli/test_vault_cmds.py::test_search_type_filter (line 176) writes a note_type="interim" note and asserts `search --type interim` returns it (the exact depth-critic discovery call); test_search_tag_filter (165) proves `--tag` filtering; test_search_query_scores_body (203) proves FTS body scoring; test_search_include_body (188) proves full-body retrieval.
4. The wiring is real: bad-research-12-critics.md:82-89 spawns all 5 critics in parallel against the synthesized report, each writing a findings JSON (critics never edit — line 7, line 114), confirming the critics are a distinct pass over a draft + a separate on-disk corpus.

The structural claim holds: a naive single-prompt Claude+WebSearch run has no separate, queryable, on-disk corpus of interim/width notes to audit its own draft against, whereas this pipeline does and the tooling to query it is real and tested.
checks.note: One honesty caveat that does NOT change the verdict: the tests verify the search TOOLING is real and the prompts INSTRUCT the searches. They cannot verify that the critic agent (an LLM at runtime) actually issues good search queries or correctly judges what counter-evidence is "missing" — that is runtime model behavior, untestable from the repo and self-graded in golden-eval (every rationale is "deterministic offline rubric"). But the auditor's claim is specifically about STRUCTURAL capability ("a single-prompt baseline structurally cannot"), and that structural fact — a separate on-disk corpus queryable independent of the draft context — is solidly confirmed by code and tests. The search scoring is naive term-frequency (vault_cmds.py:308-310), not semantic/reranked, which slightly weakens recall vs the doc's mention of FTS5/reranker, but does not refute the strength.

verdict.lens: Competitor reality-check — where bad-research genuinely beats vs loses to Grok / Gemini / OpenAI / Claude / Perplexity Deep Research, axis by axis, with file-level evidence.

strengths.claim: Keyless / zero-cost is the single most defensible competitive claim and it is real, not marketing. There is no API-key path: BuiltinProvider.search() refuses to be a search backend (web/builtin.py:66-70), the scholarly verticals send 'no key, no auth header — only a polite User-Agent' (web/search/verticals.py:1-6,18), and the calibration code physically removed the keyed Perplexity/Grok providers (calibrate/baselines.py:54-66). Versus Perplexity/Gemini/OpenAI/Grok/Claude DR — all of which are metered paid products — bad-research's marginal cost per run is the host tokens you are already paying Claude Code for. This is a genuine WIN on the cost axis and a genuine TIE-or-win on the 'runs offline/air-gapped-ish with local CLIs' angle.
strengths.evidence: web/builtin.py:66-70 (search NotImplementedError), web/search/verticals.py:1-6,18 (keyless live endpoints), calibrate/baselines.py:54-66 (keyed baselines removed), HOW_IT_WORKS.md:122-136 (keyless-vs-optional table)

strengths.claim: Persistent, inspectable, reusable vault is a real structural advantage the hosted competitors do not give you. Every fetched source becomes a SQLite/markdown note under ./research/ that compounds across sessions, and the full audit trail (loci.json, critic-findings-*.json, patch-log.json, citation-verify-actions.json) lands on your disk. Grok/Gemini/OpenAI/Perplexity DR return an opaque report inside their UI; you cannot grep their corpus or re-run a synthesis over last week's evidence. This is a defensible WIN on transparency/auditability and reusable-vault, and a partial win on controllability (you can edit prompt-decomposition.json, approve a plan-gate).
strengths.evidence: bad-research.md:184 (SQLite vault auto-init), bad-research.md:296-315 (per-step on-disk artifacts), bad-research.md:189 (archive-run audit trail), README.md:56-57 (vault compounds across sessions)

strengths.claim: Deterministic line-anchored citation grounding is the genuine quality differentiator on paper — but only the GATE is unconditionally real. The Stage-16 no-uncited-claim gate (grounding/gate.py:145-185) is a pure-string, $0, blocking ship-check that fails any non-trivial factual sentence lacking a verified cite; the recitation gate (quality/recitation.py) flags verbatim lifting with a genuine attributed-quote carve-out. No hosted competitor exposes a hard, deterministic uncited-claim ship-block you can audit. This is a real WIN on the 'cite-or-cut presence guarantee' slice — narrower than the marketing implies (see weaknesses on the NLI no-op).
strengths.evidence: grounding/gate.py:145-185 (deterministic uncited gate), grounding/gate.py:183-185 (critical findings block ship), quality/recitation.py:1-25 (recitation gate + quote carve-out), grounding/verifier.py:144-151 (Tier-A byte-identity catches fabricated/drifted quotes at $0)

strengths.claim: The 7 keyless scholarly verticals are real, live, and a legitimate primary-source breadth edge for academic/medical queries. ArxivProvider, OpenAlexProvider, CrossrefProvider, SemanticScholarProvider, EuropePMCProvider et al. are genuine HTTP clients hitting documented endpoints (export.arxiv.org/api/query, api.openalex.org/works, api.crossref.org/works) and return rich metadata (doi/year/citations/oa_pdf). For literature-review-shaped questions this is a real TIE-to-WIN vs general competitors that lean on web SERP — though Gemini (Scholar-adjacent) and OpenAI DR also reach primary literature.
strengths.evidence: web/search/verticals.py:34-93 (Arxiv/OpenAlex live endpoints + raise-safe HTTP), verticals.py:134-245 (Crossref/SemanticScholar/EuropePMC), verticals.py:1-6 (probed live 2026-05-26)

weaknesses.claim: There is ZERO head-to-head evidence that bad-research beats — or even matches — any competitor. The shipped golden-eval-report.json (pass_rate 1.0, 8/8) is fully circular: the fixtures are reports the project authored, judged by a self-authored lexical RubricJudge that BY ITS OWN ADMISSION 'CANNOT catch a well-cited claim that contradicts its source' (calibrate/golden.py:130-137) and only checks citation-presence + vocabulary overlap. Every case rationale is literally 'deterministic offline rubric' and the two adversarial fixtures (09_cited_contradiction, 10_over_hedged) carry requires_llm:true and are SKIPPED on the keyless path (golden.py:288-290). The seed set is explicitly 'built to PASS' (golden.py:26). The only real comparator, HyperresearchBaseline.run(), raises BaselineUnavailable and never executes (calibrate/baselines.py:49-51), and the keyed Perplexity/Grok baselines were deleted (baselines.py:54-66). So 'better than competitors' is unproven by any measurement in this repo.

weaknesses.claim: The '55.9 (full pipeline) vs 52.6 (single-draft fallback)' number sitting in the shipping entry skill is NOT a bad-research benchmark and NOT a competitor comparison — it is hyperresearch's old internal Q57/Q9 scores, reused without relabeling. A reader of bad-research.md:373 would reasonably take it as evidence the pipeline is empirically validated; the provenance file shows it is inherited from the upstream project's run log (docs/investigation/01_FOUNDATION.md:60 cites '(:239)' from the upstream skill). Presenting an inherited, undated, unsourced internal score as the justification for the 13-extra-Skill-invocation architecture is a defensibility gap.

weaknesses.claim: The headline 'every report sentence is checked against its source' (README.md:71, HOW_IT_WORKS.md:100-107 'Gemini grounding parity') overstates the DEFAULT keyless path. With no [local] extra and no host judge wired, the Tier-B NLI is CitationPresentNLI, which 'performs NO entailment check: every (premise, hypothesis) reads as entailment' and returns {entailment:1.0} unconditionally (grounding/verifier.py:31-41). Even when a host judge IS wired (LineSpanJudge), any claim with >=0.8 lexical overlap with the span is rubber-stamped without a judge call (verifier.py:75-79,106-113, gate.py:206) — the code itself calls the number-flip case ('grew 12%' vs 'grew 21%') a 'known keyless gap' closed only by the optional [local]/keyed lane (gate.py:196-205). So semantic entailment grounding — the thing Gemini actually does — is OPT-IN, not default. The gate guarantees a cite EXISTS and BYTE-MATCHES, not that it ENTAILS. That is a meaningful LOSE-or-tie vs Gemini/OpenAI on true semantic grounding on the out-of-the-box path.
weaknesses.evidence: grounding/verifier.py:31-41 (CitationPresentNLI no-op returns entailment=1.0), verifier.py:75-79 (>=0.8 overlap skips judge), verifier.py:116-134 (default_nli falls back to no-op when no [local]/llm), grounding/gate.py:196-206 ('known keyless gap', number-flip residual), README.md:71 vs HOW_IT_WORKS.md:100-107

weaknesses.claim: Live-web breadth & freshness is fully INHERITED, not a bad-research capability — so it cannot beat the competitors on their core axis, it ties them at best. bad-research has no search engine: BuiltinProvider.search() raises NotImplementedError telling you to 'use your agent's built-in search' (web/builtin.py:66-70). Honest verdict: LOSE-to-tie on live freshness and real-time breadth.

weaknesses.claim: Speed and scale are structurally WORSE than every commercial competitor for the marquee 'full' route. Full tier is self-described as 1.5-2.5 hours (README.md:57) running a 20-step adversarial pipeline with 13 extra Skill invocations and many serial subagent waves bounded by FETCHER_TIMEOUT_S=300 / INVESTIGATOR_TIMEOUT_S=900 (routing_constants.py:176-178). Perplexity/Grok/Gemini/OpenAI DR return comparable-depth reports in minutes. The new ultrafast route (5-15 min) is the honest answer to this, but it is NEVER auto-selected — it fires only on an explicit --ultrafast flag or literal 'ultrafast mode' phrase (bad-research.md:96-104, router.py:34 Route includes ultrafast but classify_route never emits it, router.py:183-200). So out of the box, a research-shaped question that classifies 'full' costs the user 1.5-2.5h vs minutes elsewhere — a clear LOSE on speed.

top_improvements.title: Run ONE real head-to-head: bad-research vs at least one commercial DR tool on 8-12 shared queries, human- or strong-LLM-judged, and publish the table (wins, ties, losses) with the cost delta.
top_improvements.rationale: The entire 'better than competitors' thesis currently rests on a self-graded lexical rubric over self-authored fixtures (golden.py:130-137) and an inherited 55.9/52.6 number (bad-research.md:373). A single honest comparison — even a small, caveated one — would convert the strongest marketing claim from unfalsifiable to defensible, or honestly bound it to 'we lose on speed/freshness, win on cost/auditability/vault'. Without it, a competitor-aware reader discounts every quality claim.

top_improvements.title: Relabel or qualify every quality number and grounding claim to its true scope: mark 55.9/52.6 as inherited-from-hyperresearch, and change 'every report sentence is checked against its source' to 'every cited sentence is byte-checked and gate-blocked if uncited; semantic entailment requires the optional [local]/host-judge lane'.
top_improvements.rationale: The gap between the headline (README.md:71) and the default code path (CitationPresentNLI no-op, verifier.py:31-41) is the most damaging honesty problem because grounding is the project's flagship differentiator. Competitors will be measured on real semantic grounding; overstating the default path here is the easiest claim to falsify and the most costly to credibility.

top_improvements.title: Lead the README with the axes bad-research actually wins — keyless/cost, on-disk auditable vault, deterministic uncited-claim ship-gate, keyless primary-source scholarly breadth — and explicitly concede the axes it loses (live freshness, multimodal, speed-on-full, scale).
top_improvements.rationale: The honest competitive position is narrow but real: it is the only one of these tools that is keyless, fully auditable on your own disk, and ships a deterministic citation gate. Marketing it as a general 'beats the DR tools' system invites a fair fight on freshness/multimodal/speed that it loses (web/builtin.py:66-70, no multimodal, README.md:57 1.5-2.5h). A scoped claim is both more honest and more persuasive.

top_improvements.title: Make the adversarial golden cases (cited-contradiction, over-hedge) part of the default gate instead of requires_llm-skipped, even via a cheap host-model judge, so pass_rate 1.0 stops being an artifact of skipping the hard cases.
top_improvements.rationale: golden.py:288-290 silently drops exactly the two fixtures that would test whether grounding catches a cited claim that contradicts its source — which the keyless rubric explicitly cannot do (golden.py:130-137). A pass_rate that excludes the only failure modes that matter is theater. Wiring the host LLMJudge into the gate (the seam already exists) gives the 1.0 some teeth.

verdict.competitor_notes: Axis-by-axis honest verdict (W=win, L=lose, T=tie), each marked DEFENSIBLE or MARKETING. (1) Keyless/cost: WIN, DEFENSIBLE — no key path exists (web/builtin.py:66-70, baselines.py:54-66); every commercial DR tool is metered. Strongest honest claim. (2) Persistent reusable vault: WIN, DEFENSIBLE — on-disk SQLite/markdown corpus + full audit trail (bad-research.md:184,296-315); hosted competitors give an opaque UI report. (3) Transparency/auditability: WIN, DEFENSIBLE — patch-log, critic-findings, citation-verify-actions all on disk; competitors are black boxes. (4) Line-anchored citation grounding: PARTIAL WIN, DEFENSIBLE ONLY FOR THE GATE — the deterministic uncited-claim ship-block (gate.py:145-185) and byte-identity Tier-A (verifier.py:144-151) are real and unique; but semantic entailment is a no-op on the default keyless path (verifier.py:31-41) and >=0.8-overlap is rubber-stamped (verifier.py:75-79), so on TRUE semantic grounding it's L-to-T vs Gemini/OpenAI. (5) Keyless scholarly breadth: WIN-to-TIE, DEFENSIBLE — 7 live keyless academic APIs (verticals.py:34-245); strong for lit-review, but Gemini/OpenAI DR also reach primary literature. (6) Live-web freshness & real-time breadth: LOSE-to-TIE, and the win-claim would be MARKETING — bad-research has no crawler/search engine of its own (web/builtin.py:66-70); recall is the host WebSearch tool + ddgs, freshness is prose heuristics (width-sweep.md:38,57,155). Grok's live-X firehose and Perplexity/Gemini crawlers win real-time. (7) Multimodal: HARD LOSE — zero image/vision/chart/OCR anywhere; browse is text-accessibility-tree only via an optional external CLI (agent_browser.py:1-9, HOW_IT_WORKS.md:131). Gemini/OpenAI/Grok read figures and images. (8) Speed: LOSE on the default 'full' route — 1.5-2.5h (README.md:57) vs minutes for commercial DR; the ultrafast 5-15min answer exists but is never auto-selected, explicit --ultrafast only (bad-research.md:96-104). (9) Scale: LOSE-to-TIE — single-user, serial subagent waves bounded by 300/900s timeouts (routing_constants.py:176-178); no distributed crawl/index. (10) Output polish: TIE-to-slight-LOSE — strong structural pipeline (triple-draft, synthesis, polish, readability audit) but export/ is essentially empty (only __init__.py); competitors ship formatted deliverables. (11) Controllability: WIN, DEFENSIBLE — editable decomposition, plan-gate, --effort dial, route override, on-disk everything; more knobs than any hosted UI. NET: 'better than competitors' is honest only on a NARROW slice (keyless cost + auditable vault + deterministic citation-presence gate + keyless scholarly recall + controllability). On the axes the commercial tools are actually sold on — live freshness, multimodal, speed, scale — bad-research ties at best and usually loses. There is no measurement in the repo proving a win on any axis against any named competitor; the keyed Perplexity/Grok baselines were deleted and the one remaining baseline never runs.
verdict.bottom_line: bad-research is a genuinely good engineering artifact with a narrow, defensible competitive moat and an oversold headline. Where it truly beats Grok/Gemini/OpenAI/Perplexity/Claude DR is a small slice: it is keyless (zero marginal API cost), it leaves a fully auditable, reusable corpus and audit trail on your own disk, it enforces a deterministic blocking uncited-claim ship-gate no hosted tool exposes, it reaches primary literature through 7 live keyless scholarly APIs, and it gives you more control knobs than any black-box UI — all of those are real and code-backed. Where it loses is exactly the axes those products are sold on: it has no crawler of its own (search is borrowed from the host WebSearch tool + DuckDuckGo), no multimodal/vision at all, a flagship 'full' route that takes 1.5-2.5 hours versus minutes, and no distributed scale. The honesty problems are concentrated and fixable: the flagship 'every sentence checked against its source' is a no-op on the default keyless path (the real semantic entailment is opt-in), the shipped 1.0 eval is circular self-grading over self-authored fixtures with the hard adversarial cases skipped, and the only quantitative justification in the entry skill (55.9 vs 52.6) is an inherited hyperresearch number, not a bad-research-vs-competitor benchmark. The product is not bullshit — but the claim 'better than the commercial deep-research tools' is true only on cost, auditability, vault, and control, and is unproven (and on freshness/multimodal/speed, false) everywhere else. Scope the marketing to the slice it wins and run one real head-to-head, and the project's honest position is strong.

checks.claim: There is ZERO head-to-head evidence that bad-research beats or matches any competitor: the shipped golden-eval-report.json (pass_rate 1.0, 8/8) is fully circular (self-authored fixtures, self-authored lexical RubricJudge that by its own admission cannot catch a cited contradiction and only checks citation-presence + vocabulary overlap; every rationale is 'deterministic offline rubric'); the two adversarial fixtures carry requires_llm:true and are skipped on the keyless path; the seed set is 'built to PASS'; and the only comparator (HyperresearchBaseline.run) always raises BaselineUnavailable while the keyed Perplexity/Grok baselines were deleted.

(1) golden-eval-report.json: pass_rate 1.0, total 8, and all 8 cases have verdict.rationale "deterministic offline rubric" (read in full). The 8 case ids are 01-08; 09_cited_contradiction and 10_over_hedged are ABSENT from the report. (2) calibrate/golden.py:130-137 SCOPE comment says verbatim: "the `factual` axis is a grounding-OVERLAP proxy ... NOT entailment. It catches uncited / ungrounded / overclaiming text, but it CANNOT catch a well-cited claim that *contradicts* its source (a report sharing the corpus's vocabulary passes)." The implementation confirms it: factual PASS iff content-word overlap with corpus >= 0.20 (golden.py:163-167), citation just regex-matches [N] or [[note]] (golden.py:97,151-157). (3) golden.py:26 verbatim: "The seed set is built to PASS — it is the baseline the gate defends." (4) golden.py:286-290: `if getattr(case, 'requires_llm', False) and isinstance(j, RubricJudge): continue` — adversarial fixtures skipped on the default keyless path. The one test resembling a head-to-head, test_harness.py:70-107 (test_baseline_comparison_when_available), uses a hand-written FakeBaseline returning "weaker claim" judged by a TieredJudge rigged so bad-research is all-pass and the fake all-fail (line 87 comment) — it tests delta arithmetic, not real superiority.

(b) The shipped docs never literally claim "beats competitors" — README.md:75-76 and HOW_IT_WORKS.md:140-141 only say patterns were "drawn from the best deep-research systems" (Perplexity/Gemini/etc.), and SPEC.md:203 lists competitor comparison as a future "Calibration target," not an achieved result. The owner's private memory frames it as "commercial-DR-grade" and "beat all DR tools," but that ambition is unsupported by any measurement in the repo. So "better than competitors" is unproven by any shipped measurement; the claim's substance is fully confirmed.

checks.claim: Keyless / zero-cost is the single most defensible competitive claim and it is real, not marketing — no API-key path; BuiltinProvider.search() refuses to be a search backend; the scholarly verticals are keyless; calibration removed the keyed Perplexity/Grok providers.
checks.evidence:
Every cited line checks out and the core competitive thrust (no THIRD-PARTY metered search/DR key) is real. (1) web/builtin.py:66-70 — BuiltinProvider.search() raises NotImplementedError("Builtin provider does not support web search. Use your agent's built-in search, then pipe URLs into 'hyperresearch fetch'."), verbatim as cited. (2) web/search/verticals.py:1-6 docstring "All keyless: no key, no auth header — only a polite User-Agent (+ mailto for the polite pool)"; line 18 _UA="bad-research/keyless (research tool; mailto:{mailto})". I read all 394 lines: all 7 verticals (arxiv/openalex/crossref/s2/europepmc/pubmed/wikipedia) set cost_per_search=0.0 and pass ONLY headers={"User-Agent": _UA...}; no constructor takes an api_key, no Authorization/Bearer header anywhere. WHY ONLY PARTIALLY-TRUE: the literal phrase "There is no API-key path" is an overstatement. src/bad_research/llm/anthropic.py:48 reads os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY") and raises RuntimeError if unset (line 49-53); pipeline.run_query drives this AnthropicProvider, and cli/calibrate.py:17-20 states it "still reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for the HEADLESS model calls (the only path that needs it; the skill path uses the Claude Code host model and needs no key)." So a key DOES gate the headless/calibration runner — but it is the HOST's OWN inference key for running the pipeline outside Claude Code, NOT a third-party metered search or deep-research API. The actual skill product (the keyless Claude-Code skill) supplies inference via the host and needs no key (providers.py:21 "env_var: always None in the keyless world (host supplies inference)"; line 29 anthropic-host has env_var=None). Separately, calibrate/__init__.py:5 carries a STALE docstring ("key-gated baselines hyperresearch / Perplexity / Grok when their keys are set") that contradicts the live code in baselines.py:54-66 which only registers hyperresearch — minor doc rot, not a live keyed path.
checks.note: The competitive framing the auditor builds on this (TIE/win vs Perplexity/Gemini/OpenAI/Grok/Claude-DR because those are metered third-party products and bad-research's marginal cost is the host tokens you already pay for) is sound: there is provably no third-party search/DR key anywhere in the code. The auditor should soften "There is no API-key path" to "no third-party / metered API-key path."

verdict.lens: Quality-evidence auditor: is the demonstrated quality real or circular?

strengths.claim: Tier-A byte-identity is a genuine, non-circular $0 grounding check: it kills fabricated/drifted quotes by re-slicing the live note body at [char_start:char_end] AND verifying a SHA over the quote equals the anchor_id.
strengths.evidence: grounding/verifier.py:144-151 tier_a_byte_identity recomputes quote_sha and compares note_body[char_start:char_end] == quoted_support; tests/test_grounding/test_verifier.py:41-46 proves a fabricated quote (SHA mismatch) returns False. This is real and deterministic, not self-graded.

strengths.claim: The deterministic Stage-16 uncited-claim gate (gate.py) is a real hard pass/fail with teeth: a non-trivial factual sentence with no/dangling/unverified cite blocks ship, and a recorded verify_score < 0.40 escalates to critical.
strengths.evidence: grounding/gate.py:145-185 no_uncited_claim_gate + gate_blocks_ship; severity tightening at gate.py:170-174 (verify_score < PARTIAL_LOW -> critical). is_factual_claim (gate.py:119-142) is a concrete heuristic (number/entity/comparative/causal). This is genuine $0 structural enforcement.

strengths.claim: The keyless team is unusually HONEST in code comments and SCOPE docstrings about the gap — it does not hide that the keyless factual axis is overlap-not-entailment.
strengths.evidence: golden.py:130-137 RubricJudge SCOPE: 'the factual axis is a grounding-OVERLAP proxy ... NOT entailment ... CANNOT catch a well-cited claim that contradicts its source.' gate.py:196-206 admits the number-flip residual in the >=0.8 band is 'a known keyless gap.' The honesty is in the source; the problem is the marketing/eval surface contradicts it.

strengths.claim: The NLI label-resolution code (when the [local] lane IS installed) defends against a real, subtle bug class: silent logit-order inversion.
strengths.evidence: nli.py:59-91 _label_index resolves entailment/contradiction by NAME via the model's id2label, not by hardcoded index, and classify_nli (nli.py:23-32) checks contradiction before entailment. This is correct engineering — but only live when sentence_transformers is installed (see weakness).

strengths.claim: The recitation gate is a real algorithm (word-level LCS), not a stub, with sensible carve-outs.
strengths.evidence: quality/recitation.py:48-68 longest_common_contiguous_run is a genuine DP LCS-substring; thresholds RECITATION_MAX_NGRAM=12 / overlap 0.50 at :15-16; attributed-quote and reference-line carve-outs at :92-102 and :79-89. Deterministic and verifiable.

weaknesses.claim: THE CENTRAL FINDING: on the default keyless ship path, the entailment check rubber-stamps number-flips and negation-flips. A claim with >=0.8 lexical overlap auto-returns ENTAILMENT with NO model call, even when a number or 'no' is inverted versus the cited span.
weaknesses.evidence: I ran the real code. LineSpanJudge/HostJudgeNLI (verifier.py:71-113) short-circuit to {entailment:1.0} when claim_quote_overlap >= CLAIM_QUOTE_OVERLAP_SKIP (gate.py:206 = 0.8). Empirically: quote 'grew 21 percent' vs claim 'grew 12 percent' -> overlap 0.80 -> classify_nli -> ENTAILMENT, verdict SUPPORTED. Quote 'a 20% increased risk' vs claim 'no increased risk' -> overlap 0.875 -> ENTAILMENT. The verifier then calls store.set_verified(verified=1) (verifier.py:441-444). nli.py:27's promise that 'a quote that says the OPPOSITE is never silently passed' is FALSE on this path; it holds only for the real CrossEncoder.

weaknesses.claim: The real entailment lane is dead code on the shipped machine: the [local] neural stack is not installed, so CrossEncoderNLI (the 0.70-floor, contradiction-checking lane described in SPEC §9) never runs by default.
weaknesses.evidence: importlib find_spec('sentence_transformers') -> False on this checkout. default_nli (verifier.py:128-134) therefore returns LineSpanJudge (keyless), never CrossEncoderNLI. config.py:52 neural_recall defaults False; doctor.py:116 prints 'local stack not installed (default).' So SPEC.md:154's 'NLI entailment via local nli-deberta-v3-base' describes an opt-in lane, not the default behavior most users get.
